highlights.title: OpenAI has a new $100 ChatGPT Pro plan to better match up with Claude
highlights.description:
OpenAI has closed a yawning gap in its ChatGPT subscription pricing with a new $100 per month Pro plan that slots between the $20 per month Plus plan and $200 per month Pro plan. Offering five times more Codex than the $20 option, it appears designed to challenge Anthropic's $100 per month Claude option. "Compared with Claude Code, Codex delivers more coding capacity per dollar across paid tiers," an OpenAI spokesperson told TechCrunch. 
So what's the difference between OpenAI's two Pro plans? The $200 version does offer four times the Codex. However, you get the same advanced tools and models with $100 plan, according to OpenAI's product page. To encourage users to jump in, it will offer double the Codex for a limited time, or 10 times what you get with the Plus plan. 

Users have been screaming for such a plan for a while now, according to posts on OpenAI's developer community forums. "The Plus plan will continue to be the best offer at $20 for steady, day-to-day usage of Codex, and the new $100 Pro tier offers a more accessible upgrade path for heavier daily use," OpenAI said in a post on X. 
With the launch of GPT 5.2 late last year and GPT-5.3-Codex in February, OpenAI significantly boosted the speed and reasoning capabilities of Codex, giving developers a tough choice between ChatGPT and Claude Opus. However, the sticking point for many power users was ChatGPT's $200 per month price  — so OpenAI no doubt hopes the new plan will convince those on the fence to switch. 
This article originally appeared on Engadget at https://www.engadget.com/ai/openai-has-a-new-100-chatgpt-pro-plan-to-better-match-up-with-claude-062705626.html?src=rss

highlights.title: OpenAI slams Anthropic in memo to shareholders as its leading AI rival gains momentum
highlights.description: OpenAI sent a memo to investors blasting chief rival Anthropic for "operating on a meaningfully smaller curve."

highlights.title: Anthropic Model Scare Sparks Urgent Bessent, Powell Warning to Bank CEOs
highlights.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting on concerns that the latest artificial intelligence model from Anthropic PBC will usher in an era of greater cyber risk.

highlights.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
highlights.description: The ChatGPT-maker testified in favor of an Illinois bill that would limit when AI labs can be held liable—even in cases where their products cause “critical harm.”

highlights.title: Arm CEO Says AI Is 'Much Bigger' Than the Internet Shift
highlights.description: Arm CEO Rene Haas says the artificial intelligence boom is not just another tech cycle, but rather something "much bigger" and more far-reaching than anything that came before. In an interview with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie, Haas explains why AI is scaling beyond the PC and mobile eras, driven by surging demand for compute, memory and infrastructure. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Elon Musk’s xAI Sues Colorado Over AI Anti-Discrimination Law
highlights.description: Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company xAI sued Colorado to block a new state law requiring tech companies to establish safeguards to prevent discrimination by autonomous tools in certain employment decisions, among others.

highlights.title: Arm CEO Haas on Shifting From Smartphones to AI
highlights.description: Arm CEO Rene Haas speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie as the chip company positions itself for a major shift beyond smartphones, betting big on the rapid growth of cloud computing and data centers. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: AI Boom Driving $100 Bln Chip Opportunity, Arm CEO Says
highlights.description: Chip company Arm is positioning itself for a major shift beyond smartphones, betting big on the rapid growth of cloud computing and data centers. CEO Rene Haas says the company expects this segment to dominate its business in the years ahead. Haas speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie about the lucrative opportunity. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Arm CEO on Proposed California Wealth Tax: Not a Good Thing
highlights.description: Arm CEO Rene Haas warns a proposed wealth tax in California could drive top entrepreneurs out of Silicon Valley. "I don't think it's a good thing if it drives really smart people outside the valley," Haas tells Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: China AI Firm Discloses $92 Million of Banned Nvidia Chip Servers to Beijing
highlights.description: Hours after the United States charged a Super Micro Computer Inc. co-founder with illegally smuggling billions of dollars’ worth of Nvidia Corp. AI chips to China, shares of a little-known Shenzhen-based computing company plummeted by the daily limit of 20%.

ai.title: SoftBank Group Prices ¥418 Billion Retail Bonds at Record 4.97%
ai.description: SoftBank Group Corp. priced yen hybrid bonds targeting retail investors at an initial five-year coupon of 4.97%, marking the highest level for such debt issued by the company.

ai.title: Nvidia-Backed Lumentum Sees AI Filling Orders Through 2028
ai.description: Lumentum Holdings Inc. said demand from the biggest US tech companies for its optical components is accelerating and on track to fill its order books through 2028.

ai.title: AI Stocks in Asia Lure Back Global Funds as War Tensions Ease
ai.description: As tensions in the Middle East begin to ease, global investors are turning their attention back to the artificial intelligence trade, funneling fresh funds into Asian tech stocks after weeks of selling.

ai.title: Alibaba Claims Viral Happy Horse AI Model in Latest Breakthrough
ai.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. revealed it created the “Happy Horse” video AI model that sent ripples across China’s AI industry this week, claiming ownership of a platform that topped global rankings on debut.

ai.title: Survival horror game Cronos: The New Dawn coming natively to Apple Silicon Macs
ai.description:
Bloober Team announced today that its award-winning game Cronos: The New Dawn will launch on Apple silicon Macs with native support on April 28. Here are the details.
more…

ai.title: Report: Apple saw 9% growth in Mac shipments during Q1 2026
ai.description:
A new report from IDC shows that while the overall PC market posted slight year-over-year shipment growth in the first quarter, Apple saw one of the strongest gains among its peers. Here are the details.
more…

ai.title: Adobe’s low-processing camera app expands support to select iPads and the iPhone 17e
ai.description:
The new update to Adobe’s experimental camera app adds initial support for iPads with at least 6GB of RAM and the new iPhone 17e. Here are the details.
more…

ai.title: XRP may be less exposed to quantum computer threats than bitcoin, experts say
ai.description: XRP’s design leaves a smaller share of its supply exposed to future quantum attacks than Bitcoin’s, experts said, pointing to additional XRPL features that stand out.

ai.title: Quantum-safe bitcoin now possible without a soft fork, but costs $200 a pop, new research shows
ai.description: A new scheme from a StarkWare researcher works under existing consensus rules, offering an emergency fallback while BIP-360 awaits activation.

ai.title: Mythos AI threat prompts Bessent, Powell to convene bank CEOs for urgent talks
ai.description: Mythos can rapidly spot software flaws and craft sophisticated exploits, raising fears of systemic risks in the banking system.

chips_cloud.title: TSMC’s Sales Beat Estimates After War Fails to Dent AI Demand
chips_cloud.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 35% increase in quarterly revenue, suggesting global AI chip demand remained intact during the first weeks of war in the Middle East.

chips_cloud.title: Microsoft cuts cloudy desktop prices by 20 percent, warns they’ll wake up slowly

business.title: Alibaba Leads $300 Million Bet on AI Video Platform ShengShu
business.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd.’s cloud division led a 2 billion yuan ($293 million) funding round for ShengShu Technology, bolstering the war chest of a young contender in China’s crowded AI video contest.
